rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiolative cyclization of lapachol/C-allyl-lawsone with diaryl disulfides to form fused sulfide-containing pyran (6-membered) or furan (5-membered) lapachones, followed by Ru/NaIO4 oxidation to the corresponding sulfones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSSPh to [(PhS)4]2+/[(PhS)3]+ thiiranium-type electrophile which activates the alkene; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode (paired electrolysis).

key_intermediates.name: thiiranium / sulfonium adduct on the prenyl/allyl alkene of lapachol
tertiary carbocation from Markovnikov opening (lapachol case)

electron_flow: At the anode PhSSPh is oxidized (E = -0.41 V vs Ag) to its radical cation, which dimerizes to [(PhS)4]2+ and fragments to electrophilic [(PhS)3]+; this sulfenyl cation adds to the alkene of lapachol/C-allyl-lawsone to give a sulfonium/carbocation that is intramolecularly trapped by the naphthoquinone enol-OH (6-endo for lapachol, 5-exo for less substituted C-allyl-lawsone), releasing H+ which is reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic attack of the alkene on the electrogenerated sulfenyl cation / formation of the carbocation intermediate (slower for the less substituted C-allyl-lawsone, consistent with lower yields).

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl disulfide (2a) alone
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows semi-reversible oxidation; radical cation [PhSSPh]•+ at E = -0.41 V vs Ag, evolving to [(PhS)4]2+ at -0.06 V vs Ag.

probe_reagents.name: lapachol (1) alone
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation at E = +0.70 V vs Ag; cathodic response at -1.22 V vs Ag assigned to quinone reduction/rearrangement.

probe_reagents.name: lapachol + disulfide 2a mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Lapachol oxidation peak disappears, indicating consumption/coupling with electrogenerated sulfenyl species; supports disulfide-as-mediator pathway rather than direct lapachol oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: isolated product 3a
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible reductions at -0.59 and -1.01 V and reduction at E1/2 = -0.80 V vs Ag confirm the quinone is recovered in its oxidized form after electrolysis.

notes: Mechanism is supported primarily by CV studies and product/structural (ORTEP) characterization; no radical traps, KIE, DFT, or EPR were reported. Regiochemical divergence (6-endo vs 5-exo) is rationalized by carbocation stability — lapachol's prenyl group gives a tertiary cation enabling 6-membered ring closure, while C-allyl-lawsone gives a less stabilized cation favoring 5-membered ring closure. Subsequent (non-electrochemical) RuCl3/NaIO4 oxidation converts sulfides 3/5 to sulfones 6.
